mnemonics: F.A.C.E. the Group: A group must have Four properties: Closure, Associativity, an Identity element, and an Inverse for every element.
keyPoints: A group is a set G combined with a binary operation ∗ that satisfies four axioms: closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set. The order of an element is the smallest positive integer n such that aⁿ = e (the identity).
Subgroups must themselves be groups under the same operation, meaning they must be closed, contain the identity, and contain inverses for all their elements.
commonMistakes: Assuming all operations are commutative. A group is only Abelian if its operation is commutative for all elements (a ∗ b = b ∗ a).
Forgetting to verify closure when checking if a subset is a subgroup. A subset must be closed under the group's operation to be considered a subgroup.
